title: Accessibility fines in Ireland: criminal, up to €60,000
metaDescription: Ireland is the only EU state where EAA non-compliance is criminal: up to €60,000 or 18 months, directors personally liable (S.I. 636/2023). Free scan.
ogTitle: Accessibility fines in Ireland: criminal liability
ogDescription: S.I. 636/2023: on indictment up to €60,000 or 18 months; summary up to €5,000 or 6 months. Directors are personally liable. Build your dated evidence first.
h1Html: Accessibility fines in Ireland: how bad?
answerHtml: In Ireland, non-compliance with the European Accessibility Act (S.I. 636/2023) is a criminal offence — the only EU state where that is true. On conviction on indictment: a fine up to €60,000 or 18 months' imprisonment; on summary conviction, up to €5,000 or 6 months. Company directors and officers can be personally liable unless they prove they took reasonable steps.

sections.bodyHtml: S.I. 636/2023 sets two tiers. On conviction on indictment (the higher court) the penalty is a fine up to €60,000, or imprisonment for up to 18 months, or both. On summary conviction (the District Court) it is a class-level fine up to €5,000, or up to 6 months, or both.

Most EU states enforce the EAA with administrative fines. Ireland went further: serious non-compliance is a criminal offence. That changes the risk profile — a conviction, not just a fine — and it means the matter can reach the courts rather than only a regulator.

Under Reg. 33, where an offence is committed by a company with the consent, connivance or neglect of a director, manager or officer, that individual is personally guilty of the offence — unless they can show they took reasonable steps to prevent it. Personal liability makes a dated compliance trail a board-level concern.

Bring your service to WCAG 2.1 AA, publish your accessibility statement, and keep a dated record proving you took reasonable steps — the exact defence Reg. 33 asks for. keepcite audits, fixes and documents. Start with the free scan below.

scan.sub2: Your store, scanned against the law's standard.
scan.leadHtml: Selling online in the EU? The European Accessibility Act applies to you.
scan.subject: New scan request — keepcite (IE · fines)

items.a: Enforcement is still ramping up and prosecutions take time to surface. But the criminal framework (up to €60,000 or 18 months, plus personal liability for directors) has been in force since 28 June 2025 and is available to the authorities now.

items.q: Are directors really personally at risk?
items.a: Yes. Under Reg. 33, a director, manager or officer whose consent, connivance or neglect led to the offence is personally guilty — unless they prove they took reasonable steps. A dated compliance record is that proof.

items.q: Does an accessibility widget protect me?
items.a: No. Overlays don't change the code the standard measures. 800+ professionals say they don't work, and the US FTC fined accessiBe $1M over its claims. A widget shows you knew — without fixing anything.

items.q: What should I keep as evidence?
items.a: A current accessibility statement and a dated record of conformity — audit, fixes, screen-reader tests. keepcite issues a dated certificate every month precisely for this file.
